parameters.command: =cd /Users/carlos/Brain && (echo '=== INBOX (last 24h) ===' && find inbox -type f -name '*.md' -mtime -1 -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== NOTES (last 7d) ===' && find notes -type f -name '*.md' -mtime -7 -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== IDEAS (last 7d) ===' && find ideas -type f -name '*.md' -mtime -7 -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== CLAUDE.md ===' && cat CLAUDE.md)

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "deepseek-chat",
  "max_tokens": 4000,
  "messages": [
    {"role": "system", "content": "You are reading Carlos's Obsidian Brain vault. Output ONLY valid markdown — no preamble, no code fences around the whole thing."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Below is a slice of my Brain vault: everything in /inbox from the last 24h, /notes and /ideas from the last 7d, plus my CLAUDE.md. Do three things and write them as a single markdown file:

1. CONNECTIONS — the 3 most interesting connections between recent captures and older notes I have probably not noticed. Be specific. Quote the relevant passages with the source filename.
2. PATTERN — one pattern across everything I have been reading this week. What is my brain working on even if I have not said it explicitly?
3. QUESTION — one question worth sitting with today, drawn from the pattern. Not a task, a question.

Format as:
# Daily brief — {{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}
## Connections
## Pattern
## Question

If there is too little input to give a real brief, say so plainly under each heading rather than padding.

VAULT SLICE:

{{ $json.stdout }}"}
  ]
}
